ucs.title: [LLM] Blue Mockingbird COR_PROFILER DLL hijack persistence (Monero crypto-mining loader)
ucs.description: Hunts for the actor's signature persistence trick — setting machine-wide COR_ENABLE_PROFILING / COR_PROFILER / COR_PROFILER_PATH registry values (or environment variables) so that any .NET process loads a Blue Mockingbird XMRig DLL (typically wercplsupporte.dll / wercplsupportes.dll). Red Canary observed this exact behaviour as the actor's primary boot persistence mechanism.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.registry_value_data) as registry_value_data values(Registry.process_guid) as process_guid from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\Microsoft\\.NETFramework*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Environment*") AND Registry.registry_value_name IN ("COR_ENABLE_PROFILING","COR_PROFILER","COR_PROFILER_PATH","COR_PROFILER_PATH_32","COR_PROFILER_PATH_64") by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | join type=outer process_guid [| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count values(Processes.process_name) as parent_process_name values(Processes.process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where (registry_value_name="COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" AND registry_value_data="0x00000001") OR (registry_value_name="COR_PROFILER" AND match(registry_value_data,"\{[A-F0-9\-]{36}\}")) OR (registry_value_name LIKE "COR_PROFILER_PATH%" AND NOT match(registry_value_data,"(?i)\\Program Files\\(Microsoft|dotnet|IIS)")) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Blue Mockingbird (G0108) — COR_PROFILER persistence loader
let _profiler_values = dynamic(["COR_ENABLE_PROFILING","COR_PROFILER","COR_PROFILER_PATH","COR_PROFILER_PATH_32","COR_PROFILER_PATH_64"]);
let _known_profilers = dynamic([
  @"C:\Program Files\dotnet\",
  @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\",
  @"C:\Windows\System32\inetsrv\",
  @"C:\Program Files\Microsoft Application Insights\",
  @"C:\Program Files\IIS\Microsoft Web Deploy"]);
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Microsoft\.NETFramework", @"\Environment")
| where RegistryValueName in~ (_profiler_values)
// Blue Mockingbird flips profiling on machine-wide and points it at a DLL outside the standard profiler dirs
| extend Suspicious =
    (RegistryValueName =~ "COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" and RegistryValueData == "1")
    or (RegistryValueName =~ "COR_PROFILER"        and RegistryValueData matches regex @"(?i)^\{[A-F0-9\-]{36}\}$")
    or (RegistryValueName startswith_cs "COR_PROFILER_PATH"
        and RegistryValueData endswith ".dll"
        and not(RegistryValueData has_any (_known_profilers)))
| where Suspicious
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","TrustedInstaller.exe","setup.exe","vs_installer.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Blue Mockingbird (G0108) — COR_PROFILER persistence loader
let _profiler_values = dynamic(["COR_ENABLE_PROFILING","COR_PROFILER","COR_PROFILER_PATH","COR_PROFILER_PATH_32","COR_PROFILER_PATH_64"]);
let _known_profilers = dynamic([
  @"C:\Program Files\dotnet\",
  @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\",
  @"C:\Windows\System32\inetsrv\",
  @"C:\Program Files\Microsoft Application Insights\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4657                              // registry value modified
| where ObjectName has_any (@"\Microsoft\.NETFramework", @"\Environment")
| where ObjectValueName in~ (_profiler_values)
| extend NewValue = tostring(extract(@"New Value:\s*(.+)", 1, EventData))
| extend Suspicious =
    (ObjectValueName =~ "COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" and NewValue == "1")
    or (ObjectValueName =~ "COR_PROFILER" and NewValue matches regex @"(?i)^\{[A-F0-9\-]{36}\}$")
    or (ObjectValueName startswith "COR_PROFILER_PATH"
        and NewValue endswith ".dll"
        and not(NewValue has_any (_known_profilers)))
| where Suspicious
| where ProcessName !endswith "\\msiexec.exe"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SubjectUserName, ProcessName,
          ObjectName, ObjectValueName, NewValue
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Blue Mockingbird COR_PROFILER Persistence (Monero Loader)
id: 7c3a1f0e-bm01-4fd2-9c41-corprofiler01
status: experimental
description: Detects machine-wide enabling of the .NET CLR profiler interface and registration of a non-standard COR_PROFILER DLL — Blue Mockingbird's signature persistence for their XMRig DLL loader (e.g. wercplsupporte.dll).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0108/
  - https://redcanary.com/blog/blue-mockingbird-cryptominer/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: registry_set
detection:
  selection_path:
    TargetObject|contains:
      - '\Microsoft\.NETFramework'
      - '\Environment\COR_'
  selection_value:
    TargetObject|endswith:
      - 'COR_ENABLE_PROFILING'
      - 'COR_PROFILER'
      - 'COR_PROFILER_PATH'
      - 'COR_PROFILER_PATH_32'
      - 'COR_PROFILER_PATH_64'
  filter_legit_paths:
    Details|contains:
      - 'C:\Program Files\dotnet\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\inetsrv\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft Application Insights\'
  filter_installer:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
  condition: selection_path and selection_value and not 1 of filter_*
fields:
  - Image
  - User
  - TargetObject
  - Details
falsepositives:
  - Application Insights / .NET profiling tools (filtered above)
  - APM agents (NewRelic, Dynatrace) — add to allowlist after baseline
level: high
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.012
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1112
  - attack.g0108
ucs.rationale: COR_PROFILER abuse is the single most distinguishing TTP Red Canary attributed to Blue Mockingbird's XMRig loader — the actor sets machine-wide COR_ENABLE_PROFILING=1 and points COR_PROFILER_PATH at a DLL masquerading as a Windows component (wercplsupporte.dll). Generic crypto-mining detections look at CPU usage; this catches the specific persistence implant before mining starts. The path allowlis
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Blue Mockingbird Telerik exploit → IIS worker spawning regsvr32/wmic mining DLL
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's documented initial-access-to-execution chain: exploitation of CVE-2019-18935 (Telerik UI for ASP.NET deserialization) on internet-facing IIS, where the w3wp.exe worker spawns cmd/powershell/wmic and ultimately invokes regsvr32 or rundll32 against an XMRig DLL dropped to disk. Anchors on the rare w3wp.exe→LOLBin parent/child pair plus a DLL execution within seconds.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_guid Processes.parent_process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count values(Processes.process) as dll_invoke values(Processes.process_name) as loader from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wmic.exe") (Processes.process="*werc*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*\\inetpub\\*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*process call create*xmrig*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_guid] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Blue Mockingbird (G0108) — Telerik (CVE-2019-18935) → w3wp.exe LOLBin chain → mining DLL execution
let _lookback = 7d;
let _window = 5m;
let _mining_dll_signals = dynamic([
  "wercplsupport", "wercplsupporte", "wercplsupportes",  // observed Blue Mockingbird DLL names
  "xmrig", "monero", "stratum+tcp", "--donate-level", "-o pool.", "cryptonight"]);
let _drop_paths = dynamic([
  @"\inetpub\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
// 1. w3wp.exe spawning a LOLBin (Telerik exploit yields code-exec under IIS app-pool identity)
let WebshellSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","regsvr32.exe",
                          "rundll32.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
                          "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","mshta.exe")
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              SpawnAccount = AccountName,
              SpawnedBin   = FileName,
              SpawnedCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
              IISCmd       = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// 2. regsvr32/rundll32/wmic invoking a DLL from a drop path or named like the actor's loader
let MinerInvoke = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where FileName in~ ("regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wmic.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where (ProcessCommandLine has ".dll"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any (_drop_paths))
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any (_mining_dll_signals)
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "process call create"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "regsvr32")
    | project MinerTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              MinerBin = FileName, MinerCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              MinerParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
// 3. Same host, mining invocation within 5 minutes of the IIS webshell spawn
WebshellSpawn
| join kind=inner MinerInvoke on DeviceId
| where MinerTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + _window)
| project SpawnTime, MinerTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', MinerTime, SpawnTime),
          DeviceName, SpawnAccount,
          IISCmd, SpawnedBin, SpawnedCmd,
          MinerBin, MinerCmd, MinerParent
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Blue Mockingbird (G0108) — Telerik (CVE-2019-18935) → w3wp.exe LOLBin chain → mining DLL execution
let _lookback = 7d;
let _window = 5m;
let _mining_dll_signals = dynamic([
  "wercplsupport","wercplsupporte","wercplsupportes",
  "xmrig","monero","stratum+tcp","--donate-level","cryptonight"]);
let _drop_paths = dynamic([
  @"\inetpub\",@"\Temp\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Public\",@"\Windows\Temp\"]);
let WebshellSpawn = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where NewProcessName endswith_any ("\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe",
                                         "\\regsvr32.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\wmic.exe",
                                         "\\sc.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe",
                                         "\\certutil.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\mshta.exe")
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              SpawnAccount = Account,
              SpawnedBin   = NewProcessName,
              SpawnedCmd   = CommandLine;
let MinerInvoke = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith_any
        ("\\regsvr32.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\wmic.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe")
    | where (CommandLine has ".dll" and CommandLine has_any (_drop_paths))
         or CommandLine has_any (_mining_dll_signals)
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe"
             and CommandLine has "process call create"
             and CommandLine has "regsvr32")
    | project MinerTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              MinerBin = NewProcessName, MinerCmd = CommandLine,
              MinerParent = ParentProcessName;
WebshellSpawn
| join kind=inner MinerInvoke on Computer
| where MinerTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + _window)
| project SpawnTime, MinerTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', MinerTime, SpawnTime),
          Computer, SpawnAccount,
          SpawnedBin, SpawnedCmd, MinerBin, MinerCmd, MinerParent
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: This chain — internet-facing IIS exploited via Telerik CVE-2019-18935, w3wp.exe spawning a LOLBin under the app-pool identity, then regsvr32/rundll32/wmic invoking a DLL from inetpub/Temp/ProgramData — is the exact tradecraft Red Canary published for Blue Mockingbird in May 2020. The string list (wercplsupport*, xmrig, stratum+tcp, --donate-level) is taken from the actor's actual Monero mining DLL
